content: What did the angel tell Joseph to name the child?
explanation: Scripture says: 'You shall call his name Jesus' (Matthew 1:21).

content: Where was Jesus born?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Jesus was born in Bethlehem' (Matthew 2:1).

content: When Jesus was baptized, in what form did the Spirit descend?
explanation: Scripture says: 'The Spirit of God descended on Him like a dove' (Matthew 3:16).

content: Jesus answered Satan, 'Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God,' when tempted to do what?
explanation: Scripture says: 'If you are the Son of God, command these stones to become loaves of bread' (Matthew 4:3).
options: Throw himself from the temple pinnacle

content: What is the first beatitude in the Sermon on the Mount?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven' (Matthew 5:3).
options: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness
Blessed are the poor in spirit

content: How does the Lord's Prayer 'Our Father in heaven' continue?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name. Your kingdom come, your will be done' (Matthew 6:9-10).

content: What does the name 'Immanuel' mean?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Immanuel, which means, God with us' (Matthew 1:23).

content: What did the wise men offer to Jesus?
explanation: Scripture says: 'They opened their treasures and offered him gifts: gold, frankincense, and myrrh' (Matthew 2:11).

content: When Jesus was baptized, what did the voice from heaven say?
explanation: Scripture says: 'A voice from heaven said: This is my beloved Son, with whom I am well pleased' (Matthew 3:17).

content: Jesus said to Simon and Andrew, 'Follow me, and I will make you become ___'
explanation: Scripture says: 'Follow me, and I will make you fishers of men' (Matthew 4:19).

content: Jesus says the disciples are what to the world?
explanation: Scripture says: 'You are the salt of the earth... You are the light of the world' (Matthew 5:13-14).

content: Jesus said the birds of the air do not sow, reap, or store in barns, but who feeds them?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them' (Matthew 6:26).

content: Jesus said, 'Whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them.' What is this called?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets' (Matthew 7:12).

content: When the storm rose on the sea, whom did Jesus rebuke?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Then he rose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm' (Matthew 8:26).

content: Jesus said the Son of Man has what authority on earth?
explanation: Scripture says: 'That you may know that the Son of Man has authority on earth to forgive sins' (Matthew 9:6).

content: Jesus said do not fear those who can kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Whom did He warn against?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul' (Matthew 10:28).

content: Jesus said He came to do what concerning the Law?
explanation: Scripture says: 'I have not come to abolish the Law, but to fulfill it' (Matthew 5:17).

content: Whom does Jesus invite to come to Him?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest' (Matthew 11:28).
options: All who labor and are heavy laden

content: On what did Jesus say He would build His church?
explanation: Scripture says: 'On this rock I will build my church' — referring to Peter's confession (Matthew 16:18).

content: In whose name did Jesus command His disciples to baptize?
explanation: Scripture says: 'Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit' (Matthew 28:19).
options: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit

content: The angel told Joseph to name the child Jesus (Matthew 1:21). What does the Hebrew name 'Jesus' mean?
explanation: Yeshua (ישוע) means 'the LORD saves' — the name reflects Jesus' mission.

content: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said 'Blessed are the poor in spirit' (Matthew 5:3). What does the Greek word for 'poor' mean?
explanation: Ptochos (πτωχός) means 'poor in spirit' — recognizing one's need before God.

content: What does the name 'Jesus' (Yeshua) mean?
explanation: Matthew 1:21 — Jesus (Yeshua) means 'the LORD saves.' His name reveals His purpose for coming.

content: At Jesus' baptism, how were all three Persons of the Trinity present?
explanation: Matthew 3:16-17 — Jesus (the Son) was baptized, the Holy Spirit descended like a dove, and the Father said: 'This is my beloved Son.'
options: Only the Son and the Father
The Son was baptized, the Spirit descended like a dove, the Father spoke from heaven

content: When tempted by Satan to turn stones into bread, what did Jesus answer?
explanation: Matthew 4:4 — Jesus used the Word of God (Deuteronomy 8:3) to defeat every temptation of Satan.
options: You shall not tempt the Lord
Man shall not live by bread alone, but by every word that comes from the mouth of God

content: The Beatitudes begin: 'Blessed are those who are ___'
explanation: Matthew 5:3 — Blessed are those who recognize their spiritual poverty, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.

content: What does Jesus teach about enemies?
explanation: Matthew 5:44 — This is one of Jesus' most exalted teachings, transcending ordinary morality.
options: Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you

content: How does Jesus' model prayer begin?
explanation: Matthew 6:9 — Jesus teaches us to address God as Father, expressing intimacy with Him.

content: Jesus speaks of two ways. To what does the narrow gate lead?
explanation: Matthew 7:14 — The narrow gate and hard way lead to life, and few find it. The wide gate and easy way lead to destruction.

content: Peter declared about Jesus: 'You are ___'
explanation: Matthew 16:16 — This is Peter's most important confession of faith, commended by Jesus.
options: The Christ, the Son of the living God

content: Which commandment did Jesus say was the greatest?
explanation: Matthew 22:37-38 — Loving God with all one's being is the greatest, and loving one's neighbor as oneself is the second.
options: Love the Lord with all your heart, soul, and mind

content: What is Jesus' Great Commission?
explanation: Matthew 28:19 — Go and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit.
options: Go and make disciples of all nations

content: Jesus invites: 'Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you ___'
explanation: Matthew 11:28 — Jesus invites everyone weighed down to come to Him for true rest.

content: Peter asked how many times to forgive. What did Jesus answer?
explanation: Matthew 18:22 — Forgiveness has no limit. Seventy times seven means always forgive.
